title: The One with the Girl from Poughkeepsie

jokes.text: Fifteen? Your personal best!
jokes.setup: Joey stuffing fifteen Oreos in his mouth - absurd eating behavior
jokes.punchline: Treating it like an athletic achievement worth celebrating
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey characterization - treating gluttony as accomplishment. Well-constructed with economy of words.

jokes.text: I sat near the door so she'd have to pass by me if she wanted to switch cars. She was totally at my mercy.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Describing mildly creepy behavior as strategic dating
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross overthinking - functional character comedy but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Were you so late because you were burying this woman?
jokes.setup: Ross's creepy dating strategy description
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting the logical dark conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - dark wit with excellent economy. Takes Ross's creepiness to its logical extreme.

jokes.text: How can she be great if she's from Poughkeepsie?
jokes.setup: Ross saying the girl seems great despite distance
jokes.punchline: Joey's geographic prejudice delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic - treating geography as personality flaw. Good character moment.

jokes.text: That joke would have killed in Albany.
jokes.setup: Joey's Poughkeepsie diss falling flat
jokes.punchline: Ross defending with regional humor theory
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about regional humor preferences. Clever structure and good economy.

jokes.text: Done! I did it. Who's stupid now?
jokes.setup: Phoebe working on some task
jokes.punchline: Celebrating victory over unnamed critics
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe randomness but lacks clear setup or context.

jokes.text: Who keeps leaving old newspapers in the trash?
jokes.setup: Monica pointing out paper is two weeks old
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock outrage at proper newspaper disposal
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm - pointing out the obvious absurdity of Monica's complaint.

jokes.text: I want somebody!
jokes.setup: Rachel lamenting being alone during holidays
jokes.punchline: Escalating from subtle to desperate declaration
jokes.explanation: Standard desperate single person comedy - functional but predictable.

jokes.text: You know, I want a man!
jokes.setup: Previous desperate declaration
jokes.punchline: Even more direct and primal need
jokes.explanation: Continuation of escalation - better than previous but still predictable.

jokes.text: Let me tell you, it's been a long time since I've been flung.
jokes.setup: Joey's surprise that girls want flings
jokes.punchline: Rachel using 'flung' as past tense of 'fling'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - treating 'fling' as verb with proper conjugation. Good economy.

jokes.text: I know what I'm giving you for Christmas.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying it's been long since she's been flung
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate sexual interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey sexual opportunism - predictable but well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: I have a girlfriend. I'm happy. I no longer go out of my way to stop others from being happy.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking why he never offered to set her up before
jokes.punchline: Admitting he previously sabotaged others' happiness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler self-awareness - acknowledging his past misery loves company behavior. Great character insight.

jokes.text: And Ross was what? A lion tamer?
jokes.setup: Rachel rejecting boring jobs
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Ross's equally boring paleontologist job
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's profession with sarcastic comparison. Solid structure and economy.

jokes.text: Maybe they meant to write 'Quiet, Bitch.'
jokes.setup: Monica showing hat with 'Quit, Bitch' written on it
jokes.punchline: Chandler's optimistic reinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay trying to find innocent meaning in cruel message. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: What's the matter? Fine! Just trying to be nice.
jokes.setup: Monica's negative reaction to his 'help'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's hurt feelings at rejection of terrible comfort
jokes.explanation: Chandler's obliviousness to his inappropriate 'help' - decent character moment.

jokes.text: I haven't been picked on this much since kindergarten. And they brought in someone from junior high to do the see-saw with me.
jokes.setup: Monica complaining about workplace bullying
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about specialized see-saw bullying
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd detail about childhood bullying - original imagery but slightly disconnected from character.

jokes.text: Then they would all know who was boss.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining Orson Welles' intimidation strategy
jokes.punchline: Joey treating film directing wisdom as restaurant management advice
jokes.explanation: Joey's misapplied knowledge - characteristic but not exceptional execution.

jokes.text: It's a good idea. Can you waiter? Good enough to get fired. All right. You're hired!
jokes.setup: Joey volunteering to be the sacrificial firing
jokes.punchline: Hiring someone specifically to fire them immediately
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic - Monica's standards for hiring (bad enough to fire). Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: That's why I got fired last week! Does Orson Welles direct commercials?
jokes.setup: Joey realizing he was Monica's sacrificial firing
jokes.punchline: Still confused about Orson Welles reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's acting confusion plus realization of Monica's previous manipulation.

jokes.text: I'm not asking for me. I'm not gay. I'm not asking you out. I'm not gay. I didn't think you were gay. I do now.
jokes.setup: Chandler over-clarifying he's not asking Drew out
jokes.punchline: Making it worse with each clarification
jokes.explanation: Classic escalating awkwardness - each attempt to clarify makes it worse. Good cringe structure.

jokes.text: By the way, that is her full name.
jokes.setup: Drew asking about 'Hot Rachel'
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating 'Hot' as part of her legal name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler deadpan - treating informal description as formal name. Perfect economy and delivery.

jokes.text: I don't know what that is, but let's!
jokes.setup: Drew mentioning eight-year-old Basil Haydens (whiskey)
jokes.punchline: Chandler's enthusiastic ignorance
jokes.explanation: Chandler trying to fit in with rich guy culture - good economy but predictable.

jokes.text: I know, but it's so hard. Nothing rhymes with your stupid name!
jokes.setup: Rachel pointing out dreidel doesn't rhyme with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's frustrated insult about Rachel's name
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe bluntness - blaming Rachel for her songwriting problems. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? Lots of things rhyme with Rachel: Bagel, mail, jail, bail, cable.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming nothing rhymes with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel rapid-fire listing multiple rhymes
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay demonstration but Rachel's defensiveness is predictable character response.

jokes.text: Maypole.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing things that rhyme with her name
jokes.punchline: Ross adding one more with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy - single word addition to the list with deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: But did he ever call you 'Budolph'?
jokes.setup: Trying to find nickname that rhymes better
jokes.punchline: Made-up nickname 'Budolph' to rhyme with Rudolf
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdism - inventing nonsense nickname for rhyming purposes. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Have I got the 50 guys for you!
jokes.setup: Chandler excited about setting Rachel up
jokes.punchline: Absurd number of potential dates
jokes.explanation: Chandler's enthusiasm escalating to absurd numbers - decent but predictable.

jokes.text: Guys are signing over their 401Ks to me.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing guys' reactions to Rachel's photo
jokes.punchline: Extreme financial sacrifice for dating chance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperate guy behavior - specific financial detail adds humor.

jokes.text: You work with robots?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning 401Ks at work
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confusion about workplace automation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Rachel interpreting office talk as sci-fi scenario. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: Now, wait a second. You make food and robots?
jokes.setup: Confusion about Chandler's company divisions
jokes.punchline: Rachel's continued robot obsession
jokes.explanation: Callback to robot confusion but getting repetitive - decent character consistency.

jokes.text: No, the robots just work for them.
jokes.setup: Rachel's robot company confusion
jokes.punchline: Chandler playing into the absurd interpretation
jokes.explanation: Chandler feeding into Rachel's absurd interpretation with deadpan delivery. Good economy.

jokes.text: Does anybody have a problem with that? Yeah, lady. I do! I got a problem! I'll give you a problem! What will you do? Fire me? You bet your ass I'll fire you!
jokes.setup: Monica practicing being authoritative
jokes.punchline: Joey helping with fake confrontation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - Monica and Joey rehearsing confrontation. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: If she wasn't kidding, she's not fun. She's stupid and racist.
jokes.setup: Ross unsure if date's comment was joke
jokes.punchline: Extreme interpretation of unfunny comment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from 'not fun' to 'stupid and racist' - sharp character insight about dating anxiety.

jokes.text: You're still on fire there.
jokes.setup: Monica apparently literally on fire
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual observation of dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey casualness about emergency - excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Like father, like son.
jokes.setup: Chandler being complimented on taste in men
jokes.punchline: Rachel's unintentionally sexual compliment
jokes.explanation: Good accidental innuendo - Rachel not realizing sexual implication of her compliment.

jokes.text: You telling him I want a fling and me putting out... he's so going to get the wrong idea!
jokes.setup: Rachel upset about mixed signals
jokes.punchline: Not realizing those signals actually align perfectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Rachel upset about guy getting 'wrong' idea that matches her actual behavior. Sharp writing.

jokes.text: I'd prefer you didn't call me Joey. I thought it might be fun to try out a cool nickname.
jokes.setup: Someone calling him Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey wanting cooler identity at fancy restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good Joey vanity - trying to reinvent himself in fancy environment. Character-driven but predictable.

jokes.text: Hey, Dragon!
jokes.setup: Joey wanting a cool nickname
jokes.punchline: Revealing his chosen nickname is 'Dragon'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey nickname choice - trying too hard to be cool. Great payoff to setup with good economy.

jokes.text: And it never hurts to wear tight trousers.
jokes.setup: Explaining good tip money during holidays
jokes.punchline: Joey's practical seduction advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey sexual opportunism - treating service job as pickup opportunity. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Because you'll make up fake specials and make me cook them? Sure, that too.
jokes.setup: Monica asking why servers won't write down specials
jokes.punchline: Admitting they deliberately create fake work
jokes.explanation: Good workplace sabotage reveal - servers' casual admission of malicious behavior. Well-structured.

jokes.text: Can't hear you!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to give authority speech
jokes.punchline: Staff openly ignoring her
jokes.explanation: Classic workplace disrespect comedy - simple but effective. Good economy of expression.

jokes.text: He has a name. It's Dragon.
jokes.setup: Server calling Joey 'new guy'
jokes.punchline: Monica defending Joey's ridiculous nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Monica's protective instincts for Joey. Good character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: It's been a long time since I had... $327 plus $238!
jokes.setup: Joey seeming to have emotional realization
jokes.punchline: Purely mercenary motivation about money
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - setup suggests emotional moment but reveals pure greed. Perfect Joey character moment.

jokes.text: What nice things? Nothing yet. They hate you, and I want to fit in.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what nice things he'll say
jokes.punchline: Joey's brutal honesty about situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey honesty - admitting he has nothing good to say and wants popularity over loyalty.

jokes.text: Ross doesn't decorate his tree with floss... but he's not complaining.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about not being Jewish
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarre holiday decoration reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe non-sequitur - bizarre imagery that somehow makes emotional sense. Creative absurdism.

jokes.text: It's the one with the cat.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what Phoebe's song was about
jokes.punchline: Ross's completely unhelpful clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - completely unhelpful answer delivered with Ross's matter-of-fact tone.

jokes.text: It was a different joke. It wasn't that funny.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining the uptown girl wasn't racist
jokes.punchline: Admitting her humor is still bad
jokes.explanation: Good Ross overthinking - finding new problems even when original ones are resolved.

jokes.text: Prague? So much you don't know.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning living in Prague
jokes.punchline: Mysterious hint at hidden past
jokes.explanation: Intriguing character mystery - Chandler hinting at unknown background. Good economy and intrigue.

jokes.text: You graduated magna cum laude, right?
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he didn't have to lie about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking if he lied about her education
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction of his 'no lying' claim - immediate example of potential lie. Well-structured awkwardness.

jokes.text: Actually, it's a hockey team. Angry Canadians with no teeth.
jokes.setup: Rachel expecting cute guys in shorts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's accurate but unromantic description
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character voice - Chandler's practical reality check with specific details.

jokes.text: What is the matter with you? There is no good time to ask that question.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking inappropriate question
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exasperated wisdom about timing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - universal truth about awkward questions delivered with economy.

jokes.text: I don't speak English. You just did. I don't know what to tell you.
jokes.setup: Cook claiming not to speak English
jokes.punchline: Logical contradiction pointed out and dismissed
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic - cook's ridiculous excuse and doubled-down defense. Well-structured exchange.

jokes.text: And covered in marinara sauce! Let me out!
jokes.setup: Monica trapped and cold
jokes.punchline: Additional indignity of being covered in sauce
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy escalation - adding food mess to imprisonment. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Well, that's not true.
jokes.setup: Monica saying it's not funny
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact disagreement about comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey timing and honesty - casually contradicting Monica's distress. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: What if I dance covered in sauce? You think it's funny?
jokes.setup: Monica confronting laughing staff
jokes.punchline: Threatening to perform while messy
jokes.explanation: Monica's desperate authority attempt through absurd threat. Good character desperation but predictable.

jokes.text: I bet myself that you had beautiful eyes. Now that I see them, I win!
jokes.setup: Ross waking up on train
jokes.punchline: Smooth pickup line about winning self-bet
jokes.explanation: Creative pickup line with good structure - self-betting concept is original and well-executed.

jokes.text: No, but it's only a two-hour ferry ride to Nova Scotia.
jokes.setup: Ross realizing they're in Montreal
jokes.punchline: Distance problem getting worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Ross's dating distance problem - each potential girlfriend lives further away.

jokes.text: I should have thought about my wife and kids... before I talked back to Chef Geller. It will be a lean Christmas at the Dragon house this year.
jokes.setup: Joey being fired from restaurant
jokes.punchline: Referring to himself as Dragon in third person with family
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Joey fully committed to Dragon persona even in private moments. Perfect character consistency and absurdism.

jokes.text: Lean, lean, lean!
jokes.setup: Joey saying it will be a lean Christmas
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's emphatic repetition
jokes.explanation: Weak Phoebe moment - just repeating word without clear comedic purpose or character insight.

tmdb_overview: Ross struggles with long-distance dating. Chandler sets up Rachel with a coworker. Monica fires Joey to assert authority. Phoebe writes a holiday song.
